content:
Everyone's celebrating this launch. Here's why they shouldn't be.

I spent three hours digging into the benchmarks that made headlines yesterday. What I found changes everything.

The benchmark methodology has three critical blind spots:

1. It tests on synthetic data that doesn't reflect production workloads
2. The "context window" test uses repetitive patterns, not real documents
3. Latency measurements exclude cold starts entirely

Here's the reveal: when you run real-world tasks — actual code generation, genuine document analysis — performance drops by 40%.

If your team is betting on this model, stop. Run your own evaluations on YOUR data before committing engineering resources. The benchmarks are marketing, not engineering.

What's the most misleading benchmark you've encountered? Drop it below.
